name: Inbox — Follow-up automático (24h / 48h / 72h)

nodes.name: Conversaciones sin respuesta
nodes.notes: Fetches open WhatsApp conversations where the last message was outbound (we sent, they haven't replied) and we haven't maxed out follow-ups.

nodes.name: Construir mensaje follow-up
nodes.notes: Customize follow-up text per slot here. For slot 3 the tone should be softer to avoid blocking.

nodes.name: Insertar outbox follow-up
nodes.notes: process-outbox worker will pick up this record and send via Meta. Uses free text — assumes last inbound was < 24h ago. If not, swap tipo_envio to 'template' and add template_id.

nodes.name: Continuar batch
nodes.notes: Loop back to split-conversations to process next conversation.

_notes: Logic: slot 1=24h, slot 2=48h desde follow_up_sent_at (no desde last_message_at), slot 3=72h. After 3 follow-ups, follow_up_count=3 and the Supabase filter excludes the conversation.

If the client replies (inbound), n8n auto-reply workflow sets last_message_direction='inbound'. Add a reset: when direction changes to inbound, reset follow_up_count=0 and follow_up_sent_at=null in the auto-reply workflow.
For slot 3 (last follow-up) consider using a Meta template instead of free text to ensure delivery even if the 24h window has closed.
To reset follow-ups when inbound arrives: add an Update node in inbox_auto_reply.json after 'Insert inbound message' that sets follow_up_count=0, follow_up_sent_at=null on conversations.
